breaking_news.title: Trivy Scanner Compromised in Supply Chain Attack Distributing Infostealer via GitHub Actions
breaking_news.summary:
Threat actors known as TeamPCP compromised the widely used Trivy vulnerability scanner, injecting credential-stealing malware into official releases and GitHub Actions. This supply chain attack leveraged insecure GitHub Actions workflows to distribute the malicious code to thousands of CI/CD pipelines globally.

The breach poses a severe risk to organizations relying on Trivy for security scanning, potentially exposing sensitive pipeline secrets and credentials. Security firms advise immediate rotation of all secrets and treating impacted projects as compromised to prevent a cascade of further supply chain compromises.

breaking_news.title: DarkSword Exploit Kit Targets iPhones with Zero-Days Used by State Actors
breaking_news.summary:
A new iOS exploit kit named DarkSword has been identified, utilizing six vulnerabilities including three zero-days to achieve full device compromise. Multiple threat actors, including commercial surveillance vendors and suspected nation-state groups, are actively deploying this toolkit against targets in Saudi Arabia, Turkey, and Ukraine.

Apple has urged users to update their devices immediately as the exploit chain allows attackers to steal sensitive data with minimal user interaction. This development highlights the escalating sophistication of mobile espionage campaigns and the urgent need for patching outdated iOS versions to prevent data theft.

breaking_news.title: Interlock Ransomware Exploits Cisco Firewall Zero-Day Weeks Before Patch
breaking_news.summary:
The Interlock ransomware gang exploited a critical zero-day vulnerability in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center software starting in late January. This remote code execution flaw allowed unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary Java code as root, bypassing security controls for weeks before a patch was released.

CISA has added the vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, ordering federal agencies to patch by Sunday. The active exploitation of this maximum-severity flaw underscores the urgency for organizations to apply emergency updates to prevent widespread network compromise.

breaking_news.title: WorldLeaks Ransomware Group Breaches Los Angeles Metro System
breaking_news.summary:
The WorldLeaks ransomware group launched an attack against the City of Los Angeles and its Metro system, forcing a shutdown of internal administrative systems. Unauthorized activity disrupted station arrival displays and prevented riders from adding funds to TAP cards online or via support channels.

While rail and bus services continue to run normally, the incident highlights the vulnerability of critical public infrastructure to ransomware threats. Local media reports indicate that two Bay Area cities also declared emergencies following similar ransomware attacks during the same week.

breaking_news.title: Iran-Linked Handala Group Wipes Stryker Systems via Microsoft Intune
breaking_news.summary:
Pro-Iranian threat actor Handala conducted a destructive cyberattack on medical technology giant Stryker, wiping approximately 80,000 devices. The attackers bypassed traditional malware by compromising Microsoft Intune, a legitimate endpoint management tool, to execute the destructive commands.

CISA and the FBI have issued warnings urging organizations to harden their endpoint management configurations and implement phishing-resistant authentication. This incident demonstrates how adversaries are abusing trusted enterprise infrastructure to bypass security controls and cause significant operational disruption.

breaking_news.title: PolyShell Flaw Enables Unauthenticated RCE on Thousands of Magento Sites
breaking_news.summary:
Sansec disclosed a critical vulnerability named PolyShell in the Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API that allows unauthenticated file uploads. Attackers can disguise malicious code as images to achieve remote code execution and account takeover on affected e-commerce stores.

A global campaign has already defaced over 7,500 Magento sites since late February, targeting global brands and government services. Adobe has released a fix in a pre-release version, but many stores remain vulnerable due to lack of backported patches and insecure web server configurations.

breaking_news.title: FBI Warns Russian Intelligence Targeting Signal and WhatsApp Users
breaking_news.summary:
The FBI and CISA issued a warning that Russian intelligence-linked threat actors are conducting phishing campaigns against commercial messaging applications. These attacks aim to seize control of accounts belonging to individuals with high intelligence value using encrypted apps like Signal and WhatsApp.

The campaign represents a significant escalation in state-sponsored espionage targeting secure communication channels used by high-profile individuals. Users are advised to remain vigilant against suspicious links and verify authentication requests to prevent account compromise.

breaking_news.title: International Operation Disrupts Massive IoT Botnets Behind Record DDoS Attacks
breaking_news.summary:
Authorities from the US, Germany, and Canada disrupted command-and-control infrastructure used by four major IoT botnets including Aisuru and KimWolf. These botnets compromised over three million devices and were responsible for record-breaking distributed denial-of-service attacks.

The joint law enforcement action targeted operators behind the botnets to weaken their global cybercrime activities and protect internet infrastructure. This operation highlights the ongoing threat posed by insecure IoT devices and the necessity of international cooperation to mitigate large-scale DDoS campaigns.
